title: Conjugation of GABARAP to ATG9A Compartments
author: Elisabeth Holzer
materials: Analyze proteins by SDS-PAGE using a 15% gel.
Detect GABARAP and ATG9A by Western blotting.

steps.title: Isolate ATG9A-containing compartments or MOCK preparations (from HAP1 wild-type cells)
steps.instruction: Isolate ATG9A-containing compartments or MOCK preparations (from HAP1 wild-type cells).

steps.title: Incubate each with 10 µL GFP-Trap...
steps.instruction: Incubate each with 10 µL GFP-Trap agarose beads (ChromoTek, Cat# GTA-20).

steps.title: Mix on a rotating wheel Overnight at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Mix on a rotating wheel Overnight at 4 °C .

steps.title: Wash beads twice with 200 µL Reaction Buffer
steps.instruction: Wash beads twice with 200 µL Reaction Buffer: A B 1 HEPES, pH 7.5 25 mM 2 NaCl 150 mM 3 DTT 1 mM

steps.title: Prepare a reaction mix with the...
steps.instruction: Prepare a reaction mix with the following final concentrations: A B 1 ATP 2 mM 2 MgCl₂ 1 mM 3 Human ATG12–ATG5–ATG16L1 complex 250 nM 4 Mouse ATG7 250 nM 5 Human ATG3 250 nM 6 GABARAP 500 nM Note on GABARAP: Use GABARAPΔL117 mutant (C-terminal leucine removed) for lipidation. Include full-length GABARAP as a negative control (cannot be lipidated). Include synthetic liposomes as positive control: o Lipid composition: 20.2% DOPC, 1.7% DOPS, 43.2% DOPE, 33.6% cholesterol and 1.3% sphingomyelin

steps.title: Wash beads once with 200 µL Reaction Buffer
steps.instruction: Wash beads once with 200 µL Reaction Buffer.

steps.title: Resuspend beads in SDS loading buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend beads in SDS loading buffer.
